verdicts.note: The URL https://github.com/korotovsky/slack-mcp-server points to a community GitHub repository, not evidence of an official Slack MCP server. A link to a third-party GitHub repo does not constitute 'yes' for existing_mcp; should be 'no' with no url.
This URL is a community repo, not an official MCP server from Slack. Per guidelines, we should drop the url field when exists='no'.

The original claims 'REST-style' but evidence clearly states it is 'RPC-style' not REST. Also claims 'existing community MCP servers making agent toolkits buildable today' but no evidence provided shows MCP servers exist or work. Revised to accurate terminology.
Evidence confirms Web API is broad RPC-style HTTP API with hundreds of methods, Events API, RTM, and SDKs.
Evidence confirms OAuth2 flow via oauth.v2.access and bearer token usage via Authorization header or token parameter.
Free plan pricing page confirms developers can create apps and integrate for free.
Original mentions '10-app limit' but evidence shows 'Up to 3 apps' on free tier. Corrected to reflect actual evidence.
Evidence shows self-serve API access with OAuth2, making it buildable-now for developers.

Original states 'None significant' but then lists blockers. Since API is genuinely accessible and buildable, main_blocker should be null. The notes about session tokens and admin approval are valid flags but not blockers to API buildability.

First flag about 'Community MCP servers using session tokens violate Slack ToS' is unsupported by evidence - no evidence provided about MCP servers or session token violations. Second flag about admin approval is reasonable. Revised flags to reflect actual evidence.
Increased confidence to 0.85 given strong evidence for all core claims about API, auth, and self-serve access.

revised.name: Slack
revised.one_liner: Slack provides a broad, self-serve RPC-style Web API secured via OAuth2/bearer tokens; community MCP servers exist but no official MCP support.

auth_methods.notes: Standard OAuth 2.0 flow (oauth.v2.access) to obtain bearer tokens scoped to a workspace; recommended approach for third-party apps.
Bearer tokens (including test/dev tokens) passed via Authorization header or token param; bot/user tokens generated via app configuration.

revised.self_serve_notes: Developers can create a Slack app and generate OAuth tokens for free on the Free plan to build/test integrations; some capabilities depend on the host workspace's plan (e.g., up to 3 apps on free tier, 90-day history) and commercial distribution may require additional agreements/review.

api_surface.notes: Slack Web API is a broad RPC-style HTTP API with hundreds of methods for messaging, channels, users, files, etc. Also has Events API, RTM, and SDKs.

evidence.supports: Describes Web API as RPC-style HTTP API with collection of methods
Free plan supports app development with up to 3 apps
Endpoint to check workspace plan programmatically

flags: Free plan limited to 3 apps per workspace
Official OAuth apps may need workspace admin approval for installation
